THE 2024 INVESTMENT STORY: The gap favouring MFs over FDs widened, with MF SIPs remaining the top investment choice. Interest in investment-linked insurance plans, direct equities, crypto, and even government schemes continues to decline. 3 MONEYMOOD 2025

PROVIDENT FUND (EPF/PPF): 29.0%. REAL ESTATE (RESIDENTIAL / COMMERCIAL): 24.0%. SMALL SAVINGS (KVP, NSC, SCSS, SSY, POMIS): 12.0%. GOVERNMENT SECURITIES: 12.0%. CRYPTOCURRENCY: 11.7%. CORPORATE FDS, BONDS, DEBENTURES: 11.0%. NEW AGE SAVINGS TOOLS SUCH AS P2P: 6.6%. NPS: 5.7%. FOREX: 2.0%. HAVEN'T INVESTED AT ALL: 0.9%. OTHER: 0.3%.

TOP INVESTMENTS MUTUAL FUNDS ON TOP LONG-TERM TRENDS: HOW PREFERENCES ARE SHIFTING # Instrument 2022 2023 2024 Story Behind The Trend As investors look for better returns in the light of high 1 inflation and low returns from traditional debt investments, Mutual Funds 57% 54% 62% popularity of mutual funds continues to be on the rise. and SIPs ▲ Increased education on market-linked investments has also increased investor confidence. Indian investors have always had a soft corner for FDs, given 2 Fixed & the promise of steady growth with capital protection. The Recurring 54% 53% 57% higher than usual returns have helped, but the gap between Deposits ▲ MFs and FDs has widened in this study to a record difference of 5 percent points. Surprisingly, this study has tracked a falling interest in direct 3 Stock Market investments in the stock markets, even though its overall 45% 43% 40% ▼ share remains robust. The crackdown on F&O and the relative stability of MFs may have contributed to the decline. Better education around financial products, need for higher 4 returns, and a higher risk appetite especially for long-term Life Insurance 46% 43% 37% investments is causing people to move away from ▼ endowment plans and ULIPs to more user-friendly products such as MFs. In the COVID period, small savings and EPF returns were 5 marked above the market. When the repo rate went up, PF & Postal 45% 43% 41% these products didn’t deliver higher returns. That, combined Schemes ▼ with difficulties with liquidation, may be contributing to the decline. declining interest. High taxation, lack of regulation, extreme volatility, and 6 infosecurity problems have squeezed out most interest in Cryptocurrency 32% 23% 12% this segment. However, recent geopolitical shifts and ▼ declining interest rates should revive interest, as the latest rally suggests. 5 MONEYMOOD 2025

THE FACES BEHIND THE DATA Our study surveyed 1529 respondents from 6 metros and 18+ Tier 2 cities of India. This year's study covers working professionals aged between 22 and 45 years. WOMEN 40% METROS 76% MEN 60% NON-METROS 24% SALARIED PROFESSIONALS AGED 22-45 WHO This segment forms the core of the formal workforce in India and is the primary consumer of all financial products. ARE SALARY > 30K PER MONTH Those who earn salaries in this range have the monetary bandwidth to opt for a variety of financial products including THEY? credit cards and loans. DIGITALLY AWARE They have shopped online or made a digital transaction in the recent past. It has a base of 60 million registered DVP, Communications users who use the platform for free credit score tracking and in-depth arhemant@bankbazaar.com personal finance content and comparison tools.
